clinical_history: 42-year-old woman with recent "charlie horse" in left leg, dyspnea, and syncope.  What is the leading diagnosis?
diagnosis: Acute cor pulmonale secondary to pulmonary embolism (PE).  This diagnosis is strongly suggested by the combination of sinus tachycardia, S1 Q3 T3 pattern (S wave in lead I with QR waves in III and aVF, along with T wave inversions), incomplete right bundle branch block, and right precordial T wave inversions.  The patient had bilateral pulmonary emboli secondary to deep venous thrombosis (DVT).
